body_excerpt:
ASUS received security notifications regarding third-party component vulnerabilities affecting certain chip-level products. We have confirmed that remediation is available for these vulnerabilities.
 
Affected Products：The following ASUS 4U dual-socket GPU servers featuring 5th Gen Intel Xeon Scalable processors are affected：
  • ESC8000-E11
  • ESC8000-E11P

How to Update We strongly recommend that customers keep their systems protected by applying the latest firmware. ASUS has released BIOS version 3003 to provide the necessary security patches for these servers.
  • Action: Upgrade your server BIOS to version 3003 or later. This update includes the Secure Boot DBX Update 06102025, which directly contains the fixes for the CVEs listed below.
  • Download: Please visit the "BIOS & Firmware" section on the official ASUS Support Pages to download BIOS version 3003:
    ESC8000-E11 Support Page
    ESC8000-E11P Support Page
 
Affected CVEs & References This update addresses the following Security Advisories and their associated CVEs:
  • INTEL-SA-01315：CVE-2025-20080, CVE-2025-27708, CVE-2025-32008
  • INTEL-SA-01393：CVE-2025-20096
  • INTEL-SA-01396：CVE-2025-31648
  • INTEL-SA-01397：CVE-2025-27572, CVE-2025-27940, CVE-2025-30513, CVE-2025-31944, CVE-2025-32007, CVE-2025-32467
  • INTEL-SA-01413：CVE-2025-35991
  • INSYDE-SA-2022039：CVE-2022-36337

reasons: 資産不一致または判定情報不足

title: Security Update for ASUS GPU Servers – Third-Party Component Vulnerabilities
